page: Vultr voc-g-4c-16gb-80s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 4 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 80 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance does not include dedicated GPU hardware and comes with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top tier. Single-threaded operations such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show strong performance in the top 25%. However, multi-core and multi-threaded performance, such as stress-ng multi-core and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, fall into the average and weak categories respectively, which is a typical tradeoff for shared CPU allocations. Memory write bandwidth for large blocks is strong, while read bandwidth remains average.

This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for general-purpose workloads that require high single-core burst capabilities but do not demand sustained, dedicated multi-core execution. It is well-suited for tasks like lightweight web serving, redis caching, and small-scale language model inference. It is less optimal for heavy database operations, static web serving at scale, or larger 7B parameter LLM workloads, where benchmarks show weak to poor throughput.
description: Vultr voc-g-4c-16gb-80s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 80 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this server delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks and strong single-threaded compression speeds. However, its multi-core scaling and database operations are more modest, reflecting its shared CPU allocation. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance offers a cost-effective profile for general-purpose workloads, lightweight caching, and small-scale model inference. It is less suited for sustained multi-threaded processing or high-throughput database tasks.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-4c-16gb-80s-amd is a general-purpose instance with 4 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 80 GB NVMe storage, offering top-tier single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-g-4c-16gb-80s-amd features 4 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 80 GB NVMe storage, delivering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC Genoa processors and fast local NVMe storage for single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 4 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
16.0 GB system memory and 80 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and strong single-threaded compression performance
Average multi-core performance and memory read bandwidth
Best suited for lightweight caching, redis, and small model inference
